units.content: each measurer is represented as a target entity in the storage system
property-identified attribute values are stored as directed entries written against each measurer by a further measurer
directed entries for measurers use the same six-field schema as entries recording measurements of physical targets
a first attribute set is computed comprising property identifiers of directed entries written against the first measurer as target
a second attribute set is computed comprising property identifiers of directed entries written against the second measurer as target
a symmetric difference is computed between the first attribute set and the second attribute set
a sensitivity set is maintained for each target property of interest, comprising property identifiers on which that target property is accuracy-dependent
for each target property of interest, it is determined whether the intersection of the sensitivity set and the symmetric difference is non-empty
a machine-readable divergence-prediction signal is emitted for each target property where the intersection is non-empty, identifying both measurers and the target property
the divergence-prediction signal is routed to the actor of claim 13
the emitting step produces output entirely from directed entries in storage and from sensitivity sets, without relying on external inputs
modification of an attribute-set entry for either measurer in storage causes a corresponding modification of subsequent divergence-prediction outputs
changes to divergence-prediction outputs occur without modification of the method's instructions, establishing data-driven adaptability
